assertion_results.text: The plan determines feature-branch workflow mode (not direct-to-main) based on the atomicity constraints in the feature description
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md line 4: 'Workflow Mode: feature-branch'. Lines 23-29 provide rationale based on atomicity: 'Coordinated schema migration' and 'Breaking API contract (internal)' with explanation that partial delivery would break the codebase. Line 29: 'All implementation tasks are interdependent'.

assertion_results.text: A bookend task with Bookend Type create-branch is generated as the first task in the plan
assertion_results.evidence: task-1-create-feature-branch.md contains '## Bookend Type\ncreate-branch' (lines 9-10). It is the first task file (task-1).

assertion_results.text: A bookend task with Bookend Type merge-branch is generated as the last task in the plan
assertion_results.evidence: task-7-merge-feature-branch.md contains '## Bookend Type\nmerge-branch' (lines 9-10). It is the last task file (task-7 of 7).

assertion_results.text: All intermediate tasks (between the create-branch and merge-branch bookend tasks) have Target Branch set to TC-9005
assertion_results.evidence:
Task 2 (line 8): 'Target Branch
TC-9005'. Task 3 (line 8): 'Target Branch
TC-9005'. Task 4 (line 8): 'Target Branch
TC-9005'. Task 5 (line 8): 'Target Branch
TC-9005'. Task 6 (line 8): 'Target Branch
TC-9005'. All five intermediate tasks target TC-9005.

assertion_results.text: The create-branch bookend task has Target Branch set to main
The merge-branch bookend task has Target Branch set to main
All intermediate tasks list the create-branch bookend task as a dependency
assertion_results.evidence: Task 2 (line 53): 'Depends on: Task 1 -- Create feature branch TC-9005 from main'. Task 3 (line 61): 'Depends on: Task 1 -- Create feature branch TC-9005 from main'. Task 4 (line 50): 'Depends on: Task 1 -- Create feature branch TC-9005 from main'. Task 5 (line 50): 'Depends on: Task 1 -- Create feature branch TC-9005 from main'. Task 6 (line 44): 'Depends on: Task 1 -- Create feature branch TC-9005 from main'. All five intermediate tasks list Task 1 as a dependency.

assertion_results.text: The merge-branch bookend task lists all intermediate tasks as dependencies
assertion_results.evidence: task-7-merge-feature-branch.md Dependencies section (lines 27-31) lists: Task 2, Task 3, Task 4, Task 5, Task 6. All five intermediate tasks are listed as dependencies.

assertion_results.text: Each task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1 (create-branch bookend) is missing 'Files to Modify', 'Files to Create', and 'Implementation Notes' sections. Task 7 (merge-branch bookend) is missing 'Files to Modify', 'Files to Create', and 'Implementation Notes' sections. The assertion requires 'each task file' to contain all listed sections, which includes bookend tasks. Intermediate tasks 2-6 all contain the required sections.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes a workflow:feature-branch label decision to be applied to the feature issue
assertion_results.evidence: No mention of 'workflow:feature-branch' label or any label decision found in any output file. The impact-map.md discusses the workflow mode decision but does not mention applying a label to the feature issue. Searched all 8 output files for 'label' — no matches related to workflow labels.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a real computed SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters, not a placeholder (<hex-digest>, <hex>), abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256:<64-char-hex>'
assertion_results.evidence: No SHA-256 hashes, digest comments, or '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest:' markers found in any output file. Searched all 8 output files for 'digest', 'sha256', 'sha-256', 'hex' — no matches. No evidence that description digest comments were posted for any task.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: No convention applicability rationales found in any task file. Searched all output files for 'Per CONVENTIONS', 'Applies:', 'convention' — only found generic uses of the word 'convention' in task 2 ('migration pattern') and task 6 ('project convention') which are not CONVENTIONS.md references with applicability rationale. No Implementation Notes section in any task contains the prescribed 'Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>' format.
